name: Baiji
primary_sources: Conservation Action Plan for Cetaceans of the Yangtze River

visual_attributes.morphology: streamlined body with a long, slightly upturned beak
distinctive_features: low triangular dorsal fin resembling a light-colored flag
small eyes compared to oceanic dolphins
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 2.3 to 2.7 meters in length, weighing 135–230 kilograms
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: dorsal side pale blue to grey, ventral side white

domain_specific_visual.SONAR_SYSTEM: highly directed sound emission with peak frequencies between 70 kHz and 100 kHz

functional_attributes.primary_function: navigation and social interaction using sonar

domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_ROLE: symbol of protection for local fishermen

relationships.description: Native to the Yangtze river system in China
Closest known relative from the Miocene and Pliocene

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Middle and lower reaches of the Yangtze River
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Regarded as a goddess of protection by local fishermen
primary_sources: Conservation Action Plan for Cetaceans of the Yangtze River
